mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts signals a weakening supply cycle that reduces the risk of overcapacity in the housing market, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or HOUST does not decline below 550k units within the 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or FEDFUNDS does not rise above 2.5% within the 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply cycle that reduces the risk of overcapacity in the manufacturing sector, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO does not decline below 95 within the 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market that boosts risk aversion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or UNRATE does not rise above 6% within the 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real personal income signals a strengthening demand cycle that boosts consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
